## Backlog Close-Out Notes

If this pull request implements a feature request or backlog issue, include
enough detail for the issue timeline to remain useful after merge. Do not close
the live issue until the release containing the work has actually been
published; use `Related #` for normal pre-release implementation work.

- What shipped:
- What changed for users or operators:
- Tests/checks run:
- Documentation and changelog updates:
- Known limitations or follow-up issues:
- Planned release tag:
- Acceptance criteria status:
- Test plan evidence:
- Close-out evidence:

## Checklist

- [ ] Every user-visible change is linked to a GitHub issue or explains why no issue was needed.
- [ ] Feature requests are assigned, have a sanitized implementation note, and have a concrete release label before close-out.
- [ ] Feature requests have all Acceptance Criteria checked before the associated release is published.
- [ ] Feature requests have sanitized Test Plan Evidence and Close-Out Evidence comments before release automation closes them.
- [ ] Feature requests are closed only after the containing release is published.
- [ ] Commit-then-acknowledge semantics are preserved.
- [ ] Unit tests do not make network calls.
- [ ] Integration tests are marked when added.
- [ ] Public behavior changes are documented.
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` is updated for user-visible changes.
- [ ] Secrets and payloads are not logged by default.
